Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»BREAKING: Akani Simbine MISSES out on 100m medal

South Africa’s Akani Simbine missed out on a medal in the final of the men’s 100m at the Paris Olympics on Sunday evening.

South Africa’sAkani Simbinenarrowly missed out on a medal in the final of the men’s100mat theParis Olympicson Sunday evening.

Simbine clocked9.82– a new South African record – but finished fourth.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

AmericanNoah Lylestook the gold medal in a personal best of 9.79, ahead of Jamaica’sKishane Thompson(9.79), withFred Kerley(United States) in third (9.81).

Simbine was the lone South African to have qualified for the final of the blue riband event of this year’s athletics competition at the Paris Olympics.

There will be three Americans, two Jamaicans and one runner from South Africa, Botswana and Italy in the eight-man medal-decider.

Lane 1 will be occupied by AmericanKenny Bednarekwith his compatriotFred Kerleyin Lane 2.Kishane Thompsonof Jamaica will start in Lane 3 with Simbine in the next lane followed by Jamaica’sOblique Sevillein Lane 5.

Lanes 6-8 will be filled by AmericanNoah Lyles,Letsile Tebogoof Botswana and defending 100m Olympic champion,Lamont Jacobsof Italy, respectively.

Earlier, running in the second of three semi-finals, Simbine finished first in a time of9.87to book his spot in the medal-decider.

Meanwhile,Shaun Maswanganyifinished fifth in the first semi-final in10:02, whileBenjamin Richardsonfinished third in the third semi-final in9.95, but both failed to progress to the final.

Simbine finished fourth in the 100m in Tokyo in 2021 and fifth in Rio de Janeiro in 2016.

title: BREAKING: Akani Simbine MISSES out on 100m medal
author: Garrin Lambley
description: South Africa's Akani Simbine missed out on a medal in the final of the men's 100m at the Paris Olympics on Sunday evening.
